scope_note: QA Witt Tower Acoustic Speech Spectral Entropy Orbit Discriminator cert [451]. Fifth domain in Witt tower empirical chain: acoustic speech phoneme discrimination. Feature: spectral entropy H_norm = -sum(p_i log2 p_i)/log2(64); same formula as cert [450]. Data: source-filter model synthesis (Fant 1960); voiced /a/ = harmonic stack F0~120 Hz (jitter +/-3 Hz) with 1/n^2 glottal rolloff + random phases; 80 frames x 25 ms = 2 s. Unvoiced /s/ = Gaussian noise bandlimited 3-8 kHz via 4th-order Butterworth; 200 frames x 25 ms = 5 s. numpy seed per frame, deterministic. Feature: scipy.signal.welch (nperseg=128, noverlap=64) single channel; use bins 1-64 (exclude DC); normalize to probability mass; H_norm = H/log2(64). Theorem NT: synthesized acoustic waveform (Pa, float) is observer projection; PSD and H_norm are observer layer; rank bin in Z/27Z is QA integer state. CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) 80 voiced + 200 unvoiced = 280 windows PASS. (C2) mean_voiced_H=0.163 < 0.75 x mean_unvoiced_H=0.887 (threshold 0.666) PASS. (C3) ALL 80 voiced in T0 (Singularity); hypergeometric log10_p=-55.23 PASS. (C4) mean_unvoiced=0.887 - mean_voiced=0.163 = 0.724 > 0.60 PASS. (C5) Voiced tier set={T0}; unvoiced spans {T0,T1,T2} (14/93/93) PASS. (C6) Relative entropy reduction 81.6% >= 75% PASS. 6 checks PASS; 8/8 fixtures PASS. PRIMARY SOURCES: Fant G (1960) Acoustic Theory of Speech Production (source-filter model); Shannon CE (1948) doi:10.1002/j.1538-7305.1948.tb01338.x (spectral entropy); Wall HS (1960) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Witt tower theory). Structural parent: cert [110]. Empirical chain: certs [442]-[450]. Fifth domain: acoustic speech. Validated 2026-06-18.
